column3: In this objective, 3G is treated as an older generation that improved mobile data capability compared with earlier cellular service.
column4:
Memory Trick

Use the generation number as the recall anchor; do not over-focus on exact performance numbers.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.4; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 1.3

column3: LTE is identified in the approved source as a 4G cellular technology.
column4:
Exam Trap

OpenAPlus does not turn the source's exact LTE rate examples into cards; the exam value here is recognizing LTE as a cellular generation term.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.4; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 1.3

column2: 5G is the {{c1::fifth generation}} of cellular networking.
column3: The approved source emphasizes higher performance goals and additional IoT impact for 5G.
column4:
Source Note

Avoid adding unsupported claims about exact current carrier coverage or device support.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.4; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 1.3

column3: A SIM identifies a cellular device to the carrier and can contain carrier and user information.
column4:
Memory Trick

“Identity” is the key word: the SIM identifies the subscriber/device relationship to the cellular carrier.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.4; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 1.3
column9: A+::220-1201::1.3 A+::220-1201::MobileDeviceNetworks Cloze HighYield SIM Acronym

column3: MDM centrally manages mobile-device policies, settings, access control, and application restrictions.
column4:
Why

MDM is the enterprise control layer for company-owned and user-owned mobile devices.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.5; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 1.3
column9: A+::220-1201::1.3 A+::220-1201::MobileDeviceNetworks Cloze HighYield MDM Acronym

column3: In BYOD, the employee owns the device but must meet the organization's requirements.
column4:
Exam Trap

BYOD is attractive for flexibility, but it complicates security because personal and company data share the same device.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.5; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 1.3

column3: In COPE, the organization owns and controls the device while allowing personal use.
column4:
Related Cards

1.3-B010 tests applying COPE to a scenario; this card tests acronym recall.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.5; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 1.3

column3: CYOD is similar to COPE, but the user chooses from approved device options.
column4:
Exam Trap

CYOD still keeps the organization inside the device-selection process; it is not the same as unrestricted BYOD.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.5; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 1.3
